parentQuestion: The phrenic nerve branches from which plexus?

scaffolds.q: The diaphragm is the main muscle used for breathing.
scaffolds.explain: The diaphragm is a dome-shaped muscle that separates the chest from the abdomen. When it contracts, it flattens and pulls air into the lungs — the primary engine of respiration. Without it, breathing becomes incredibly difficult. I want you to appreciate the elegance of this mechanism...

scaffolds.q: The cervical plexus is formed from neck-level spinal nerves.
scaffolds.explain: The cervical plexus is formed from spinal nerves C1-C4 in the neck. It supplies muscles of the neck and — critically — the diaphragm via the phrenic nerve. this is why high spinal cord injuries are so devastating. Sit with this knowledge:drawn into the implications...
